era: 1970s-present. Hard Rock. E=6 -- the guitar, the composition, the film score. Seattle. Solo career separate from Heart (234-235).
born: March 16, 1954, San Francisco, California (raised Seattle, WA)
members: Nancy Wilson (guitar, vocals, composition, film scoring)
role_in_heart: Lead guitarist (acoustic + electric). Co-composer. Structural/compositional intelligence of the band.
distinctive_from_ann_wilson: Nancy = guitar, composition, film scoring, acoustic fingerpicking. Ann = vocals, belt, emotional center. E=6 vs E=7. Different second careers: film scoring vs. cover albums.

guitar_technique.acoustic: Fingerpicking tradition -- classical and folk influence. Independent right-hand (bass + melody simultaneously). Complex chord voicings. Crazy on You intro as exemplar.
guitar_technique.electric: Rhythm-focused. Harmonic foundation supporting Ann Wilson's voice. Professional and solid; acoustic work is exceptional.
guitar_technique.range: Full range from intimate arpeggio to hard rock power chord -- uncommon flexibility in rock guitarists.
guitar_technique.compositional_role: Nancy's guitar work is compositional, not decorative. She determines structure, arrangement, dynamic trajectory of Heart songs.

film_scoring.title: Almost Famous
film_scoring.award: Film won Academy Award for Best Original Screenplay
film_scoring.notes: Wilson's original score provides emotional connective tissue between licensed tracks (Zeppelin, Who, Elton John). Guitar-centered emotional architecture.

film_scoring.title: Vanilla Sky
film_scoring.notes: Sci-fi psychological thriller. More atmospheric and unsettling than Almost Famous. Demonstrates compositional range.

theory_nodes.concept: WILSON SISTERS COMPLETE. Degrees 259 (Ann) and 260 (Nancy) form the complete Wilson sisters portrait. Heart (234-235) documented the band as a unit. Individual entries document two distinct careers, two distinct instruments, two distinct contributions. Ann: vocals, belt, emotional center, E=7. Nancy: guitar, composition, film scoring, E=6. The E difference (7 vs 6) captures something true about their roles.
theory_nodes.description: Wilson sisters individual documentation complete at degrees 259-260.

theory_nodes.concept: Acoustic guitar as compositional instrument in hard rock. Nancy Wilson's acoustic work (Crazy on You intro, Dog & Butterfly) draws on classical and folk fingerpicking -- independent right-hand movement, complex chord voicings -- not rock rhythm guitar. The acoustic guitar is made structural, not decorative. Unusual at commercial hard rock scale in 1975-1978. No comparable integration in contemporaries.
theory_nodes.description: Acoustic fingerpicking as architectural element in commercial hard rock.

theory_nodes.concept: STORM ARC COMPLETE (256-260). Nancy Wilson at degree 260 (E=6) paired with Lightning Strike Tree (E=9, NEW ABSOLUTE SPS MAXIMUM). The Storm Arc built through four consecutive E=8 SPS readings (256-259) to the absolute maximum at 260. E=9 is the highest energy reading in the Sound of Puget Sound engine. Nancy Wilson's patient compositional architecture paired with the most instantaneous and violent natural sound in the engine.
theory_nodes.description: Storm Arc complete. E=9 absolute maximum arrives at degree 260.

cross_references.relationship: Band entry. Nancy and Ann together. Full Heart arc from Mushroom Records to Capitol Records. Nancy's guitar work in band context.
The voice (E=7). Belt technique. Emotional center. Back-to-back individual documentation. 259+260 together = complete Wilson sisters portrait.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=6 vs E=9. NEW ABSOLUTE SPS MAXIMUM. Patient architectural composition vs. instantaneous irreversible violence. Nancy Wilson's long guitar figure and film score arc (structure over time) against the lightning bolt (total event, no duration). The patient and the instant at the same moment.

storm_arc_position.note: Final S36 entry of Storm Arc. SPS closes at E=9 ABSOLUTE MAXIMUM.

milestones: SPS E=9 ABSOLUTE MAXIMUM ARRIVES AT PAIR
